tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""
Step 3: Feature Engineering
Transform survey demographic data into numeric features for causal analysis
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder

print("="*80)
print("STEP 3: FEATURE ENGINEERING")
print("="*80)

# ============================================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================================
print("\n1. Loading cleaned survey data:")

output_dir = Path('/app/output')
survey_path = output_dir / 'survey_cleaned.csv'
survey_df = pd.read_csv(survey_path)

print(f"   Rows: {len(survey_df)}")
print(f"   Columns: {len(survey_df.columns)}")
print(f"   Survey ResponseIDs: {survey_df['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")

# Preview data
print("\n   Column names:")
for col in survey_df.columns:
    print(f"   - {col}")

# ============================================================================
# SEPARATE ID FROM FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n2. Separating Survey ResponseID from features:")

response_id = survey_df['Survey ResponseID'].copy()
demo_features = survey_df.drop('Survey ResponseID', axis=1).copy()

print(f"   Features to engineer: {len(demo_features.columns)}")

# ============================================================================
# DEFINE FEATURE TRANSFORMATIONS
# ============================================================================
print("\n3. Categorizing features by type:")

# Binary features (Yes/No)
binary_simple = [
    'Q-demos-hispanic',
    'Q-personal-diabetes',
    'Q-personal-wheelchair'
]

# Substance use features (Yes/No/Prefer not to say/I stopped...)
substance_features = [
    'Q-substance-use-cigarettes',
    'Q-substance-use-marijuana',
    'Q-substance-use-alcohol'
]

# Ordinal features (with natural ordering)
ordinal_features = {
    'Q-demos-age': [
        '18 - 24 years',
        '25 - 34 years',
        '35 - 44 years',
        '45 - 54 years',
        '55 - 64 years',
        '65 and older'
    ],
    'Q-demos-education': [
        'Some high school or less',
        'High school diploma or GED',
        'Bachelor\'s degree',
        'Graduate or professional degree (MA, MS, MBA, PhD, JD, MD, DDS, etc)',
        'Prefer not to say'
    ],
    'Q-demos-income': [
        'Less than $25,000',
        '$25,000 - $49,999',
        '$50,000 - $74,999',
        '$75,000 - $99,999',
        '$100,000 - $149,999',
        '$150,000 or more',
        'Prefer not to say'
    ],
    'Q-amazon-use-howmany': [
        '1 (just me!)',
        '2',
        '3',
        '4+'
    ],
    'Q-amazon-use-hh-size': [
        '1 (just me!)',
        '2',
        '3',
        '4+'
    ],
    'Q-amazon-use-how-oft': [
        'Less than 5 times per month',
        '5 - 10 times per month',
        'More than 10 times per month'
    ]
}

# Nominal features (no natural order - one-hot encode)
nominal_features = [
    'Q-demos-race',
    'Q-demos-gender',
    'Q-sexual-orientation',
    'Q-demos-state'
]

print(f"   Binary (simple Yes/No): {len(binary_simple)}")
print(f"   Substance use (multi-level): {len(substance_features)}")
print(f"   Ordinal: {len(ordinal_features)}")
print(f"   Nominal: {len(nominal_features)}")

# ============================================================================
# TRANSFORM BINARY FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n4. Transforming binary features:")

for col in binary_simple:
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        # Map Yes to 1, No to 0
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].map({'Yes': 1, 'No': 0})
        # Fill any remaining NaN with 0 (shouldn't happen after cleaning, but safety check)
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].fillna(0).astype(int)
        print(f"   ✓ {col}: Yes/No → 1/0")

# ============================================================================
# TRANSFORM SUBSTANCE USE FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n5. Transforming substance use features:")

for col in substance_features:
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        # Check unique values
        unique_vals = demo_features[col].unique()
        print(f"   {col}: {len(unique_vals)} unique values")

        # Create binary: currently using (Yes) vs not currently using
        demo_features[f'{col}_current'] = (demo_features[col] == 'Yes').astype(int)

        # Create binary: ever used (Yes or stopped)
        demo_features[f'{col}_ever'] = (
            (demo_features[col] == 'Yes') |
            (demo_features[col] == 'I stopped in the recent past')
        ).astype(int)

        # Create prefer not to say indicator
        demo_features[f'{col}_undisclosed'] = (
            demo_features[col] == 'Prefer not to say'
        ).astype(int)

        # Drop original column
        demo_features = demo_features.drop(col, axis=1)
        print(f"   ✓ Created: {col}_current, {col}_ever, {col}_undisclosed")

# ============================================================================
# TRANSFORM ORDINAL FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n6. Transforming ordinal features:")

for col, order in ordinal_features.items():
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        # Create ordered categorical
        demo_features[col] = pd.Categorical(
            demo_features[col],
            categories=order,
            ordered=True
        )

        # Convert to codes (0, 1, 2, ...)
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].cat.codes

        # Handle any -1 (missing category) - shouldn't happen after cleaning
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].replace(-1, 0)

        print(f"   ✓ {col}: encoded as ordinal (0-{len(order)-1})")

# ============================================================================
# ONE-HOT ENCODE NOMINAL FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n7. One-hot encoding nominal features:")

for col in nominal_features:
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        unique_count = demo_features[col].nunique()
        print(f"   {col}: {unique_count} unique values")

        # One-hot encode
        dummies = pd.get_dummies(demo_features[col], prefix=col, drop_first=False)

        # Convert boolean to int
        dummies = dummies.astype(int)

        # Drop original column and add dummies
        demo_features = demo_features.drop(col, axis=1)
        demo_features = pd.concat([demo_features, dummies], axis=1)

        print(f"   ✓ Created {len(dummies.columns)} one-hot features")

# ============================================================================
# VALIDATE ALL FEATURES ARE NUMERIC
# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Validating feature transformations:")

# Check data types
non_numeric_cols = []
for col in demo_features.columns:
    if demo_features[col].dtype not in ['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']:
        non_numeric_cols.append(col)

if len(non_numeric_cols) > 0:
    print(f"   ERROR: Found non-numeric columns: {non_numeric_cols}")
    for col in non_numeric_cols:
        print(f"   - {col}: {demo_features[col].dtype}")
        print(f"     Sample values: {demo_features[col].head().tolist()}")
    raise ValueError("All features must be numeric!")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ All {len(demo_features.columns)} features are numeric")

# Check for missing values
missing_counts = demo_features.isnull().sum()
if missing_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found missing values:")
    for col, count in missing_counts[missing_counts > 0].items():
        print(f"   - {col}: {count}")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No missing values")

# Check for constant features
constant_features = []
for col in demo_features.columns:
    if demo_features[col].nunique() == 1:
        constant_features.append(col)

if len(constant_features) > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found {len(constant_features)} constant features (will be removed):")
    for col in constant_features[:5]:
        print(f"   - {col}")
    # Remove constant features
    demo_features = demo_features.drop(constant_features, axis=1)
    print(f"   ✓ Removed {len(constant_features)} constant features")

# Check for infinite values
infinite_counts = np.isinf(demo_features.select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum()
if infinite_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found infinite values")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No infinite values")

# ============================================================================
# COMBINE AND SAVE
# ============================================================================
print("\n9. Creating final output:")

# Combine Survey ResponseID with engineered features
output_df = pd.concat([response_id.reset_index(drop=True),
                       demo_features.reset_index(drop=True)], axis=1)

print(f"   Total columns: {len(output_df.columns)}")
print(f"   Survey ResponseID column: 1")
print(f"   Feature columns: {len(demo_features.columns)}")
print(f"   Total rows: {len(output_df)}")

# Final validation
feature_cols = [c for c in output_df.columns if c != 'Survey ResponseID']
numeric_cols = output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']).columns
print(f"\n   Final validation:")
print(f"   - Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Numeric features: {len(numeric_cols)}")
print(f"   - All numeric: {len(feature_cols) == len(numeric_cols)}")

# Save output
output_path = output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv'
output_df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {output_path}")

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n10. Feature summary:")

print(f"\n   Sample feature names (first 20):")
for i, col in enumerate(feature_cols[:20], 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {col}")

if len(feature_cols) > 20:
    print(f"   ... and {len(feature_cols) - 20} more features")

print(f"\n   Feature statistics:")
print(f"   - Total features: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Binary features (0/1): {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Min unique values: {min(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Max unique values: {max(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nINPUT:")
print(f"  - Cleaned survey data: {len(survey_df)} respondents")
print(f"  - Original demographic columns: {len(survey_df.columns) - 1}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT:")
print(f"  - File: {output_path}")
print(f"  - Rows: {len(output_df)}")
print(f"  - Survey ResponseID: 1 column")
print(f"  - Engineered features: {len(feature_cols)} columns")
print(f"  - All features numeric: ✓")

print(f"\nFEATURE TYPES:")
print(f"  - Binary/indicator features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"  - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")

print(f"\nDATA QUALITY:")
print(f"  - Missing values: {output_df[feature_cols].isnull().sum().sum()}")
print(f"  - Infinite values: {np.isinf(output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum().sum()}")
print(f"  - Constant features: 0 (removed)")

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("✓ Feature engineering completed successfully!")
print("="*80)

        demo_features = demo_features.drop(col, axis=1)
        demo_features = pd.concat([demo_features, dummies], axis=1)

        print(f"   ✓ Created {len(dummies.columns)} one-hot features")

# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Validating feature transformations:")

# Check data types
non_numeric_cols = []
for col in demo_features.columns:
    if demo_features[col].dtype not in ['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']:
        non_numeric_cols.append(col)

if len(non_numeric_cols) > 0:
    print(f"   ERROR: Found non-numeric columns: {non_numeric_cols}")
    for col in non_numeric_cols:
        print(f"   - {col}: {demo_features[col].dtype}")
        print(f"     Sample values: {demo_features[col].head().tolist()}")
    raise ValueError("All features must be numeric!")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ All {len(demo_features.columns)} features are numeric")

# Check for missing values
missing_counts = demo_features.isnull().sum()
if missing_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found missing values:")
    for col, count in missing_counts[missing_counts > 0].items():
        print(f"   - {col}: {count}")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No missing values")

# Check for constant features
constant_features = []
for col in demo_features.columns:
    if demo_features[col].nunique() == 1:
        constant_features.append(col)

if len(constant_features) > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found {len(constant_features)} constant features (will be removed):")
    for col in constant_features[:5]:
        print(f"   - {col}")
    # Remove constant features
    demo_features = demo_features.drop(constant_features, axis=1)
    print(f"   ✓ Removed {len(constant_features)} constant features")

# Check for infinite values
infinite_counts = np.isinf(demo_features.select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum()
if infinite_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found infinite values")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No infinite values")

# ============================================================================
# COMBINE AND SAVE
# ============================================================================
print("\n9. Creating final output:")

# Combine Survey ResponseID with engineered features
output_df = pd.concat([response_id.reset_index(drop=True),
                       demo_features.reset_index(drop=True)], axis=1)

print(f"   Total columns: {len(output_df.columns)}")
print(f"   Survey ResponseID column: 1")
print(f"   Feature columns: {len(demo_features.columns)}")
print(f"   Total rows: {len(output_df)}")

# Final validation
feature_cols = [c for c in output_df.columns if c != 'Survey ResponseID']
numeric_cols = output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']).columns
print(f"\n   Final validation:")
print(f"   - Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Numeric features: {len(numeric_cols)}")
print(f"   - All numeric: {len(feature_cols) == len(numeric_cols)}")

# Save output
output_path = output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv'
output_df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {output_path}")

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n10. Feature summary:")

print(f"\n   Sample feature names (first 20):")
for i, col in enumerate(feature_cols[:20], 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {col}")

if len(feature_cols) > 20:
    print(f"   ... and {len(feature_cols) - 20} more features")

print(f"\n   Feature statistics:")
print(f"   - Total features: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Binary features (0/1): {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Min unique values: {min(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Max unique values: {max(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nINPUT:")
print(f"  - Cleaned survey data: {len(survey_df)} respondents")
print(f"  - Original demographic columns: {len(survey_df.columns) - 1}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT:")
print(f"  - File: {output_path}")
print(f"  - Rows: {len(output_df)}")
print(f"  - Survey ResponseID: 1 column")
print(f"  - Engineered features: {len(feature_cols)} columns")
print(f"  - All features numeric: ✓")

print(f"\nFEATURE TYPES:")
print(f"  - Binary/indicator features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"  - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")

print(f"\nDATA QUALITY:")

print(f"  - Infinite values: {np.isinf(output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum().sum()}")

"""
Step 3: Feature Engineering
Transform survey demographic data into numeric features for causal analysis
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder

print("="*80)
print("STEP 3: FEATURE ENGINEERING")
print("="*80)

# ============================================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================================
print("\n1. Loading cleaned survey data:")

output_dir = Path('/app/output')
survey_path = output_dir / 'survey_cleaned.csv'
survey_df = pd.read_csv(survey_path)

print(f"   Rows: {len(survey_df)}")
print(f"   Columns: {len(survey_df.columns)}")
print(f"   Survey ResponseIDs: {survey_df['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")

# Preview data
print("\n   Column names:")
for col in survey_df.columns:
    print(f"   - {col}")

# ============================================================================
# SEPARATE ID FROM FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n2. Separating Survey ResponseID from features:")

response_id = survey_df['Survey ResponseID'].copy()
demo_features = survey_df.drop('Survey ResponseID', axis=1).copy()

print(f"   Features to engineer: {len(demo_features.columns)}")

# ============================================================================
# DEFINE FEATURE TRANSFORMATIONS
# ============================================================================
print("\n3. Categorizing features by type:")

# Binary features (Yes/No)
binary_simple = [
    'Q-demos-hispanic',
    'Q-personal-diabetes',
    'Q-personal-wheelchair'
]

# Substance use features (Yes/No/Prefer not to say/I stopped...)
substance_features = [
    'Q-substance-use-cigarettes',
    'Q-substance-use-marijuana',
    'Q-substance-use-alcohol'
]

# Ordinal features (with natural ordering)
ordinal_features = {
    'Q-demos-age': [
        '18 - 24 years',
        '25 - 34 years',
        '35 - 44 years',
        '45 - 54 years',
        '55 - 64 years',
        '65 and older'
    ],
    'Q-demos-education': [
        'Some high school or less',
        'High school diploma or GED',
        'Bachelor\'s degree',
        'Graduate or professional degree (MA, MS, MBA, PhD, JD, MD, DDS, etc)',
        'Prefer not to say'
    ],
    'Q-demos-income': [
        'Less than $25,000',
        '$25,000 - $49,999',
        '$50,000 - $74,999',
        '$75,000 - $99,999',
        '$100,000 - $149,999',
        '$150,000 or more',
        'Prefer not to say'
    ],
    'Q-amazon-use-howmany': [
        '1 (just me!)',
        '2',
        '3',
        '4+'
    ],
    'Q-amazon-use-hh-size': [
        '1 (just me!)',
        '2',
        '3',
        '4+'
    ],
    'Q-amazon-use-how-oft': [
        'Less than 5 times per month',
        '5 - 10 times per month',
        'More than 10 times per month'
    ]
}

# Nominal features (no natural order - one-hot encode)
nominal_features = [
    'Q-demos-race',
    'Q-demos-gender',
    'Q-sexual-orientation',
    'Q-demos-state'
]

print(f"   Binary (simple Yes/No): {len(binary_simple)}")
print(f"   Substance use (multi-level): {len(substance_features)}")
print(f"   Ordinal: {len(ordinal_features)}")
print(f"   Nominal: {len(nominal_features)}")

# ============================================================================
# TRANSFORM BINARY FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n4. Transforming binary features:")

for col in binary_simple:
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        # Map Yes to 1, No to 0
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].map({'Yes': 1, 'No': 0})
        # Fill any remaining NaN with 0 (shouldn't happen after cleaning, but safety check)
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].fillna(0).astype(int)
        print(f"   ✓ {col}: Yes/No → 1/0")

# ============================================================================
# TRANSFORM SUBSTANCE USE FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n5. Transforming substance use features:")

for col in substance_features:
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        # Check unique values
        unique_vals = demo_features[col].unique()
        print(f"   {col}: {len(unique_vals)} unique values")

        # Create binary: currently using (Yes) vs not currently using
        demo_features[f'{col}_current'] = (demo_features[col] == 'Yes').astype(int)

        # Create binary: ever used (Yes or stopped)
        demo_features[f'{col}_ever'] = (
            (demo_features[col] == 'Yes') |
            (demo_features[col] == 'I stopped in the recent past')
        ).astype(int)

        # Create prefer not to say indicator
        demo_features[f'{col}_undisclosed'] = (
            demo_features[col] == 'Prefer not to say'
        ).astype(int)

        # Drop original column
        demo_features = demo_features.drop(col, axis=1)
        print(f"   ✓ Created: {col}_current, {col}_ever, {col}_undisclosed")

# ============================================================================
# TRANSFORM ORDINAL FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n6. Transforming ordinal features:")

for col, order in ordinal_features.items():
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        # Create ordered categorical
        demo_features[col] = pd.Categorical(
            demo_features[col],
            categories=order,
            ordered=True
        )

        # Convert to codes (0, 1, 2, ...)
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].cat.codes

        # Handle any -1 (missing category) - shouldn't happen after cleaning
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].replace(-1, 0)

        # Convert to standard int
        demo_features[col] = demo_features[col].astype(int)

        print(f"   ✓ {col}: encoded as ordinal (0-{len(order)-1})")

# ============================================================================
# ONE-HOT ENCODE NOMINAL FEATURES
# ============================================================================
print("\n7. One-hot encoding nominal features:")

for col in nominal_features:
    if col in demo_features.columns:
        unique_count = demo_features[col].nunique()
        print(f"   {col}: {unique_count} unique values")

        # One-hot encode
        dummies = pd.get_dummies(demo_features[col], prefix=col, drop_first=False)

        # Convert boolean to int
        dummies = dummies.astype(int)

        # Drop original column and add dummies
        demo_features = demo_features.drop(col, axis=1)
        demo_features = pd.concat([demo_features, dummies], axis=1)

        print(f"   ✓ Created {len(dummies.columns)} one-hot features")

# ============================================================================
# VALIDATE ALL FEATURES ARE NUMERIC
# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Validating feature transformations:")

# Check data types
non_numeric_cols = []
for col in demo_features.columns:
    if demo_features[col].dtype not in ['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']:
        non_numeric_cols.append(col)

if len(non_numeric_cols) > 0:
    print(f"   ERROR: Found non-numeric columns: {non_numeric_cols}")
    for col in non_numeric_cols:
        print(f"   - {col}: {demo_features[col].dtype}")
        print(f"     Sample values: {demo_features[col].head().tolist()}")
    raise ValueError("All features must be numeric!")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ All {len(demo_features.columns)} features are numeric")

# Check for missing values
missing_counts = demo_features.isnull().sum()
if missing_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found missing values:")
    for col, count in missing_counts[missing_counts > 0].items():
        print(f"   - {col}: {count}")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No missing values")

# Check for constant features
constant_features = []
for col in demo_features.columns:
    if demo_features[col].nunique() == 1:
        constant_features.append(col)

if len(constant_features) > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found {len(constant_features)} constant features (will be removed):")
    for col in constant_features[:5]:
        print(f"   - {col}")
    # Remove constant features
    demo_features = demo_features.drop(constant_features, axis=1)
    print(f"   ✓ Removed {len(constant_features)} constant features")

# Check for infinite values
infinite_counts = np.isinf(demo_features.select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum()
if infinite_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found infinite values")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No infinite values")

# ============================================================================
# COMBINE AND SAVE
# ============================================================================
print("\n9. Creating final output:")

# Combine Survey ResponseID with engineered features
output_df = pd.concat([response_id.reset_index(drop=True),
                       demo_features.reset_index(drop=True)], axis=1)

print(f"   Total columns: {len(output_df.columns)}")
print(f"   Survey ResponseID column: 1")
print(f"   Feature columns: {len(demo_features.columns)}")
print(f"   Total rows: {len(output_df)}")

# Final validation
feature_cols = [c for c in output_df.columns if c != 'Survey ResponseID']
numeric_cols = output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']).columns
print(f"\n   Final validation:")
print(f"   - Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Numeric features: {len(numeric_cols)}")
print(f"   - All numeric: {len(feature_cols) == len(numeric_cols)}")

# Save output
output_path = output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv'
output_df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {output_path}")

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n10. Feature summary:")

print(f"\n   Sample feature names (first 20):")
for i, col in enumerate(feature_cols[:20], 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {col}")

if len(feature_cols) > 20:
    print(f"   ... and {len(feature_cols) - 20} more features")

print(f"\n   Feature statistics:")
print(f"   - Total features: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Binary features (0/1): {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Min unique values: {min(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Max unique values: {max(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nINPUT:")
print(f"  - Cleaned survey data: {len(survey_df)} respondents")
print(f"  - Original demographic columns: {len(survey_df.columns) - 1}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT:")
print(f"  - File: {output_path}")
print(f"  - Rows: {len(output_df)}")
print(f"  - Survey ResponseID: 1 column")
print(f"  - Engineered features: {len(feature_cols)} columns")
print(f"  - All features numeric: ✓")

print(f"\nFEATURE TYPES:")
print(f"  - Binary/indicator features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"  - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")

print(f"\nDATA QUALITY:")
print(f"  - Missing values: {output_df[feature_cols].isnull().sum().sum()}")
print(f"  - Infinite values: {np.isinf(output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum().sum()}")
print(f"  - Constant features: 0 (removed)")

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("✓ Feature engineering completed successfully!")
print("="*80)

    if demo_features[col].nunique() == 1:
        constant_features.append(col)

if len(constant_features) > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found {len(constant_features)} constant features (will be removed):")
    for col in constant_features[:5]:
        print(f"   - {col}")
    # Remove constant features
    demo_features = demo_features.drop(constant_features, axis=1)
    print(f"   ✓ Removed {len(constant_features)} constant features")

# Check for infinite values
infinite_counts = np.isinf(demo_features.select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum()
if infinite_counts.sum() > 0:
    print(f"   WARNING: Found infinite values")
else:
    print(f"   ✓ No infinite values")

# ============================================================================
# COMBINE AND SAVE
# ============================================================================
print("\n9. Creating final output:")

# Combine Survey ResponseID with engineered features
output_df = pd.concat([response_id.reset_index(drop=True),
                       demo_features.reset_index(drop=True)], axis=1)

print(f"   Total columns: {len(output_df.columns)}")
print(f"   Survey ResponseID column: 1")
print(f"   Feature columns: {len(demo_features.columns)}")
print(f"   Total rows: {len(output_df)}")

# Final validation
feature_cols = [c for c in output_df.columns if c != 'Survey ResponseID']
numeric_cols = output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=['int64', 'float64', 'int32', 'float32']).columns
print(f"\n   Final validation:")
print(f"   - Feature count: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Numeric features: {len(numeric_cols)}")
print(f"   - All numeric: {len(feature_cols) == len(numeric_cols)}")

# Save output
output_path = output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv'
output_df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {output_path}")

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n10. Feature summary:")

print(f"\n   Sample feature names (first 20):")
for i, col in enumerate(feature_cols[:20], 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {col}")

if len(feature_cols) > 20:
    print(f"   ... and {len(feature_cols) - 20} more features")

print(f"\n   Feature statistics:")
print(f"   - Total features: {len(feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Binary features (0/1): {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Min unique values: {min(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"   - Max unique values: {max(output_df[col].nunique() for col in feature_cols)}")

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nINPUT:")
print(f"  - Cleaned survey data: {len(survey_df)} respondents")
print(f"  - Original demographic columns: {len(survey_df.columns) - 1}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT:")
print(f"  - File: {output_path}")
print(f"  - Rows: {len(output_df)}")
print(f"  - Survey ResponseID: 1 column")
print(f"  - Engineered features: {len(feature_cols)} columns")
print(f"  - All features numeric: ✓")

print(f"\nFEATURE TYPES:")
print(f"  - Binary/indicator features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() == 2 for col in feature_cols)}")
print(f"  - Multi-level features: {sum(output_df[col].nunique() > 2 for col in feature_cols)}")

print(f"\nDATA QUALITY:")
print(f"  - Missing values: {output_df[feature_cols].isnull().sum().sum()}")
print(f"  - Infinite values: {np.isinf(output_df[feature_cols].select_dtypes(include=[np.number])).sum().sum()}")
print(f"  - Constant features: 0 (removed)")

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("✓ Feature engineering completed successfully!")
print("="*80)

arguments.content:
"""
Step 4: Prepare Data for DiD Analysis
Create user-category-period aggregated data for intensive and extensive margin analysis
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

print("="*80)
print("STEP 4: PREPARE DATA FOR DiD ANALYSIS")
print("="*80)

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================
print("\n1. Configuration:")

output_dir = Path('/app/output')

# Time periods
baseline_start = '2020-01-01'
baseline_end = '2020-02-29'
treatment_start = '2020-03-01'
treatment_end = '2020-03-31'

print(f"   Baseline period: {baseline_start} to {baseline_end}")
print(f"   Treatment period: {treatment_start} to {treatment_end}")

# ============================================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================================
print("\n2. Loading data:")

# Load anomaly index
anomaly_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'category_anomaly_index.csv')
print(f"   Anomaly index: {len(anomaly_df)} categories")

# Load cleaned purchases
purchase_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'amazon-purchases-2019-2020-filtered.csv')
purchase_df['Order Date'] = pd.to_datetime(purchase_df['Order Date'])
print(f"   Purchases: {len(purchase_df)} transactions")

# Load engineered features
features_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv')
print(f"   Features: {len(features_df)} respondents, {len(features_df.columns)-1} features")

# ============================================================================
# IDENTIFY TOP SURGE AND SLUMP CATEGORIES
# ============================================================================
print("\n3. Identifying top surge and slump categories:")

# Sort by anomaly index descending
anomaly_df = anomaly_df.sort_values('Anomaly_Index', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# Top 10 surge categories (highest positive anomaly)
top_surge = anomaly_df.head(10)['Category'].tolist()
print(f"\n   Top 10 SURGE categories:")
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(anomaly_df.head(10).iterrows(), 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {row['Category']:40s} (Index: {row['Anomaly_Index']:6.2f})")

# Bottom 10 slump categories (lowest/most negative anomaly)
bottom_slump = anomaly_df.tail(10)['Category'].tolist()
print(f"\n   Bottom 10 SLUMP categories:")
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(anomaly_df.tail(10).iloc[::-1].iterrows(), 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {row['Category']:40s} (Index: {row['Anomaly_Index']:6.2f})")

# Combine for analysis
target_categories = top_surge + bottom_slump
print(f"\n   Total target categories for DiD analysis: {len(target_categories)}")

# ============================================================================
# FILTER PURCHASES TO TARGET PERIOD AND CATEGORIES
# ============================================================================
print("\n4. Filtering purchases to target period and categories:")

# Filter to Jan-March 2020
period_purchases = purchase_df[
    (purchase_df['Order Date'] >= baseline_start) &
    (purchase_df['Order Date'] <= treatment_end)
].copy()

print(f"   Purchases in Jan-Mar 2020: {len(period_purchases)}")

# Filter to target categories
target_purchases = period_purchases[
    period_purchases['Category'].isin(target_categories)
].copy()

print(f"   Purchases in target categories: {len(target_purchases)}")

# Calculate total spend per transaction
target_purchases['Total_Spend'] = (
    target_purchases['Purchase Price Per Unit'] * target_purchases['Quantity']
)

# Assign period labels
def assign_period(date):
    if baseline_start <= date <= baseline_end:
        return 'baseline'
    elif treatment_start <= date <= treatment_end:
        return 'treatment'
    else:
        return 'other'

target_purchases['Period'] = target_purchases['Order Date'].apply(
    lambda x: assign_period(x.strftime('%Y-%m-%d'))
)

# Verify periods
print(f"\n   Purchases by period:")
period_counts = target_purchases['Period'].value_counts()
for period, count in period_counts.items():
    print(f"   - {period}: {count}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE INTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Total_Spend)
# ============================================================================
print("\n5. Creating intensive margin aggregated data:")

# Aggregate total spend per user-category-period
intensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).agg({
    'Total_Spend': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

print(f"   Intensive margin records: {len(intensive_agg)}")
print(f"   Unique users: {intensive_agg['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   Unique categories: {intensive_agg['Category'].nunique()}")

# Fill in missing user-category-period combinations with 0 spend
# First, create all possible combinations
all_users = features_df['Survey ResponseID'].unique()
all_periods = ['baseline', 'treatment']

# Create full grid
intensive_full = []
for user in all_users:
    for category in target_categories:
        for period in all_periods:
            intensive_full.append({
                'Survey ResponseID': user,
                'Category': category,
                'Period': period
            })

intensive_full_df = pd.DataFrame(intensive_full)

# Merge with actual data
intensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    intensive_agg,
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Total_Spend with 0
intensive_final['Total_Spend'] = intensive_final['Total_Spend'].fillna(0)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"   Zero-spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Non-zero spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

# Save intensive margin data
intensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_intensive.csv'
intensive_final.to_csv(intensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {intensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE EXTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Has_Purchase)
# ============================================================================
print("\n6. Creating extensive margin aggregated data:")

# Create binary indicator for whether user purchased in category-period
extensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).size().reset_index(name='transaction_count')

extensive_agg['Has_Purchase'] = 1

print(f"   Extensive margin records (actual purchases): {len(extensive_agg)}")

# Merge with full grid
extensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    extensive_agg[['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period', 'Has_Purchase']],
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Has_Purchase with 0
extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] = extensive_final['Has_Purchase'].fillna(0).astype(int)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"   No purchase (0): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Has purchase (1): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 1).sum()}")

# Save extensive margin data
extensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_extensive.csv'
extensive_final.to_csv(extensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {extensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# VERIFY DATA STRUCTURE
# ============================================================================
print("\n7. Verifying data structure:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {intensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {intensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {intensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {intensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2} = {len(all_users)} users × {len(target_categories)} categories × 2 periods")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(intensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample intensive data:")
print(intensive_final.head(10))

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {extensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {extensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {extensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {extensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(extensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample extensive data:")
print(extensive_final.head(10))

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Summary statistics:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN (Total_Spend):")
intensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'median', 'sum', 'std'])
print(intensive_by_period)

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN (Has_Purchase rate):")
extensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Has_Purchase'].agg(['mean', 'sum'])
print(extensive_by_period)

# By category type
surge_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(top_surge)]
slump_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(bottom_slump)]

print(f"\n   SURGE CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(surge_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

print(f"\n   SLUMP CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(slump_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("DATA PREPARATION COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nTARGET CATEGORIES:")
print(f"  - Surge categories: {len(top_surge)}")
print(f"  - Slump categories: {len(bottom_slump)}")
print(f"  - Total: {len(target_categories)}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT FILES:")
print(f"  - Intensive margin: {intensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\n  - Extensive margin: {extensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\nDATA STRUCTURE:")
print(f"  - Users: {len(all_users)}")
print(f"  - Categories: {len(target_categories)}")
print(f"  - Periods: 2 (baseline, treatment)")
print(f"  - Expected rows per file: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")

print(f"\nPURCHASE BEHAVIOR:")
print(f"  - Baseline purchases: {(intensive_final[intensive_final['Period'] == 'baseline']['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

Step 4: Prepare Data for DiD Analysis
Create user-category-period aggregated data for intensive and extensive margin analysis
"""

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

print("="*80)
print("STEP 4: PREPARE DATA FOR DiD ANALYSIS")
print("="*80)

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================
print("\n1. Configuration:")

output_dir = Path('/app/output')

# Time periods
baseline_start = '2020-01-01'
baseline_end = '2020-02-29'
treatment_start = '2020-03-01'
treatment_end = '2020-03-31'

print(f"   Baseline period: {baseline_start} to {baseline_end}")
print(f"   Treatment period: {treatment_start} to {treatment_end}")

# ============================================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================================
print("\n2. Loading data:")

# Load anomaly index
anomaly_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'category_anomaly_index.csv')
print(f"   Anomaly index: {len(anomaly_df)} categories")

# Load cleaned purchases
purchase_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'amazon-purchases-2019-2020-filtered.csv')
purchase_df['Order Date'] = pd.to_datetime(purchase_df['Order Date'])
print(f"   Purchases: {len(purchase_df)} transactions")

# Load engineered features
features_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv')
print(f"   Features: {len(features_df)} respondents, {len(features_df.columns)-1} features")

# ============================================================================
# IDENTIFY TOP SURGE AND SLUMP CATEGORIES
# ============================================================================
print("\n3. Identifying top surge and slump categories:")

# Sort by anomaly index descending
anomaly_df = anomaly_df.sort_values('Anomaly_Index', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# Top 10 surge categories (highest positive anomaly)
top_surge = anomaly_df.head(10)['Category'].tolist()
print(f"\n   Top 10 SURGE categories:")
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(anomaly_df.head(10).iterrows(), 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {row['Category']:40s} (Index: {row['Anomaly_Index']:6.2f})")

# Bottom 10 slump categories (lowest/most negative anomaly)
bottom_slump = anomaly_df.tail(10)['Category'].tolist()
print(f"\n   Bottom 10 SLUMP categories:")
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(anomaly_df.tail(10).iloc[::-1].iterrows(), 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {row['Category']:40s} (Index: {row['Anomaly_Index']:6.2f})")

# Combine for analysis
target_categories = top_surge + bottom_slump
print(f"\n   Total target categories for DiD analysis: {len(target_categories)}")

# ============================================================================
# FILTER PURCHASES TO TARGET PERIOD AND CATEGORIES
# ============================================================================
print("\n4. Filtering purchases to target period and categories:")

# Filter to Jan-March 2020
period_purchases = purchase_df[
    (purchase_df['Order Date'] >= baseline_start) &
    (purchase_df['Order Date'] <= treatment_end)
].copy()

print(f"   Purchases in Jan-Mar 2020: {len(period_purchases)}")

# Filter to target categories
target_purchases = period_purchases[
    period_purchases['Category'].isin(target_categories)
].copy()

print(f"   Purchases in target categories: {len(target_purchases)}")

# Calculate total spend per transaction
target_purchases['Total_Spend'] = (
    target_purchases['Purchase Price Per Unit'] * target_purchases['Quantity']
)

# Assign period labels
def assign_period(date):
    if baseline_start <= date <= baseline_end:
        return 'baseline'
    elif treatment_start <= date <= treatment_end:
        return 'treatment'
    else:
        return 'other'

target_purchases['Period'] = target_purchases['Order Date'].apply(
    lambda x: assign_period(x.strftime('%Y-%m-%d'))
)

# Verify periods
print(f"\n   Purchases by period:")
period_counts = target_purchases['Period'].value_counts()
for period, count in period_counts.items():
    print(f"   - {period}: {count}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE INTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Total_Spend)
# ============================================================================
print("\n5. Creating intensive margin aggregated data:")

# Aggregate total spend per user-category-period
intensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).agg({
    'Total_Spend': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

print(f"   Intensive margin records: {len(intensive_agg)}")
print(f"   Unique users: {intensive_agg['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   Unique categories: {intensive_agg['Category'].nunique()}")

# Fill in missing user-category-period combinations with 0 spend
# First, create all possible combinations
all_users = features_df['Survey ResponseID'].unique()
all_periods = ['baseline', 'treatment']

# Create full grid
intensive_full = []
for user in all_users:
    for category in target_categories:
        for period in all_periods:
            intensive_full.append({
                'Survey ResponseID': user,
                'Category': category,
                'Period': period
            })

intensive_full_df = pd.DataFrame(intensive_full)

# Merge with actual data
intensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    intensive_agg,
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Total_Spend with 0
intensive_final['Total_Spend'] = intensive_final['Total_Spend'].fillna(0)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"   Zero-spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Non-zero spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

# Save intensive margin data
intensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_intensive.csv'
intensive_final.to_csv(intensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {intensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE EXTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Has_Purchase)
# ============================================================================
print("\n6. Creating extensive margin aggregated data:")

# Create binary indicator for whether user purchased in category-period
extensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).size().reset_index(name='transaction_count')

extensive_agg['Has_Purchase'] = 1

print(f"   Extensive margin records (actual purchases): {len(extensive_agg)}")

# Merge with full grid
extensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    extensive_agg[['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period', 'Has_Purchase']],
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Has_Purchase with 0
extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] = extensive_final['Has_Purchase'].fillna(0).astype(int)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"   No purchase (0): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Has purchase (1): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 1).sum()}")

# Save extensive margin data
extensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_extensive.csv'
extensive_final.to_csv(extensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {extensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# VERIFY DATA STRUCTURE
# ============================================================================
print("\n7. Verifying data structure:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {intensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {intensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {intensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {intensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2} = {len(all_users)} users × {len(target_categories)} categories × 2 periods")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(intensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample intensive data:")
print(intensive_final.head(10))

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {extensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {extensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {extensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {extensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(extensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample extensive data:")
print(extensive_final.head(10))

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Summary statistics:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN (Total_Spend):")
intensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'median', 'sum', 'std'])
print(intensive_by_period)

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN (Has_Purchase rate):")
extensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Has_Purchase'].agg(['mean', 'sum'])
print(extensive_by_period)

# By category type
surge_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(top_surge)]
slump_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(bottom_slump)]

print(f"\n   SURGE CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(surge_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

print(f"\n   SLUMP CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(slump_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("DATA PREPARATION COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nTARGET CATEGORIES:")
print(f"  - Surge categories: {len(top_surge)}")
print(f"  - Slump categories: {len(bottom_slump)}")
print(f"  - Total: {len(target_categories)}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT FILES:")
print(f"  - Intensive margin: {intensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\n  - Extensive margin: {extensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\nDATA STRUCTURE:")
print(f"  - Users: {len(all_users)}")
print(f"  - Categories: {len(target_categories)}")
print(f"  - Periods: 2 (baseline, treatment)")
print(f"  - Expected rows per file: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")

print(f"\nPURCHASE BEHAVIOR:")
print(f"  - Baseline purchases: {(intensive_final[intensive_final['Period'] == 'baseline']['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")
print(f"  - Treatment purchases: {(intensive_final[intensive_final['Period'] == 'treatment']['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

print("\n" + "="*80)
print("✓ Data preparation completed successfully!")
print("="*80)

print(f"   Purchases in Jan-Mar 2020: {len(period_purchases)}")

# Filter to target categories
target_purchases = period_purchases[
    period_purchases['Category'].isin(target_categories)
].copy()

print(f"   Purchases in target categories: {len(target_purchases)}")

# Calculate total spend per transaction
target_purchases['Total_Spend'] = (
    target_purchases['Purchase Price Per Unit'] * target_purchases['Quantity']
)

# Assign period labels
def assign_period(date):
    if baseline_start <= date <= baseline_end:
        return 'baseline'
    elif treatment_start <= date <= treatment_end:
        return 'treatment'
    else:
        return 'other'

target_purchases['Period'] = target_purchases['Order Date'].apply(
    lambda x: assign_period(x.strftime('%Y-%m-%d'))
)

# Verify periods
print(f"\n   Purchases by period:")
period_counts = target_purchases['Period'].value_counts()
for period, count in period_counts.items():
    print(f"   - {period}: {count}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE INTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Total_Spend)
# ============================================================================
print("\n5. Creating intensive margin aggregated data:")

# Aggregate total spend per user-category-period
intensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).agg({
    'Total_Spend': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

print(f"   Intensive margin records: {len(intensive_agg)}")
print(f"   Unique users: {intensive_agg['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   Unique categories: {intensive_agg['Category'].nunique()}")

# Fill in missing user-category-period combinations with 0 spend
# First, create all possible combinations
all_users = features_df['Survey ResponseID'].unique()
all_periods = ['baseline', 'treatment']

# Create full grid
intensive_full = []
for user in all_users:
    for category in target_categories:
        for period in all_periods:
            intensive_full.append({
                'Survey ResponseID': user,
                'Category': category,
                'Period': period
            })

intensive_full_df = pd.DataFrame(intensive_full)

# Merge with actual data
intensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    intensive_agg,
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Total_Spend with 0
intensive_final['Total_Spend'] = intensive_final['Total_Spend'].fillna(0)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"   Zero-spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Non-zero spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

# Save intensive margin data
intensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_intensive.csv'
intensive_final.to_csv(intensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {intensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE EXTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Has_Purchase)
# ============================================================================
print("\n6. Creating extensive margin aggregated data:")

# Create binary indicator for whether user purchased in category-period
extensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).size().reset_index(name='transaction_count')

extensive_agg['Has_Purchase'] = 1

print(f"   Extensive margin records (actual purchases): {len(extensive_agg)}")

# Merge with full grid
extensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    extensive_agg[['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period', 'Has_Purchase']],
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Has_Purchase with 0
extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] = extensive_final['Has_Purchase'].fillna(0).astype(int)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"   No purchase (0): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Has purchase (1): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 1).sum()}")

# Save extensive margin data
extensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_extensive.csv'
extensive_final.to_csv(extensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {extensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# VERIFY DATA STRUCTURE
# ============================================================================
print("\n7. Verifying data structure:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {intensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {intensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {intensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {intensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2} = {len(all_users)} users × {len(target_categories)} categories × 2 periods")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(intensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample intensive data:")
print(intensive_final.head(10))

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {extensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {extensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {extensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {extensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(extensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample extensive data:")
print(extensive_final.head(10))

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Summary statistics:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN (Total_Spend):")
intensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'median', 'sum', 'std'])
print(intensive_by_period)

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN (Has_Purchase rate):")
extensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Has_Purchase'].agg(['mean', 'sum'])
print(extensive_by_period)

# By category type
surge_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(top_surge)]
slump_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(bottom_slump)]

print(f"\n   SURGE CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(surge_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

print(f"\n   SLUMP CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(slump_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("DATA PREPARATION COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nTARGET CATEGORIES:")
print(f"  - Surge categories: {len(top_surge)}")
print(f"  - Slump categories: {len(bottom_slump)}")
print(f"  - Total: {len(target_categories)}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT FILES:")
print(f"  - Intensive margin: {intensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\n  - Extensive margin: {extensive_path}")

print(f"    Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\nDATA STRUCTURE:")
print(f"  - Users: {len(all_users)}")
print(f"  - Categories: {len(target_categories)}")
print(f"  - Periods: 2 (baseline, treatment)")
print(f"  - Expected rows per file: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")

print(f"\nPURCHASE BEHAVIOR:")
print(f"  - Baseline purchases: {(intensive_final[intensive_final['Period'] == 'baseline']['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")
print(f"  - Treatment purchases: {(intensive_final[intensive_final['Period'] == 'treatment']['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

"""

import pandas as pd

print("="*80)

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION

print("\n1. Configuration:")

output_dir = Path('/app/output')

# Time periods
baseline_start = '2020-01-01'
baseline_end = '2020-02-29'
treatment_start = '2020-03-01'
treatment_end = '2020-03-31'

print(f"   Baseline period: {baseline_start} to {baseline_end}")
print(f"   Treatment period: {treatment_start} to {treatment_end}")

# ============================================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================================
print("\n2. Loading data:")

# Load anomaly index
anomaly_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'category_anomaly_index.csv')
print(f"   Anomaly index: {len(anomaly_df)} categories")

# Load cleaned purchases
purchase_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'amazon-purchases-2019-2020-filtered.csv')
purchase_df['Order Date'] = pd.to_datetime(purchase_df['Order Date'])
print(f"   Purchases: {len(purchase_df)} transactions")

# Load engineered features
features_df = pd.read_csv(output_dir / 'survey_feature_engineered.csv')
print(f"   Features: {len(features_df)} respondents, {len(features_df.columns)-1} features")

# ============================================================================
# IDENTIFY TOP SURGE AND SLUMP CATEGORIES
# ============================================================================
print("\n3. Identifying top surge and slump categories:")

# Sort by anomaly index descending
anomaly_df = anomaly_df.sort_values('Anomaly_Index', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# Top 10 surge categories (highest positive anomaly)
top_surge = anomaly_df.head(10)['Category'].tolist()
print(f"\n   Top 10 SURGE categories:")
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(anomaly_df.head(10).iterrows(), 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {row['Category']:40s} (Index: {row['Anomaly_Index']:6.2f})")

# Bottom 10 slump categories (lowest/most negative anomaly)
bottom_slump = anomaly_df.tail(10)['Category'].tolist()
print(f"\n   Bottom 10 SLUMP categories:")
for i, (idx, row) in enumerate(anomaly_df.tail(10).iloc[::-1].iterrows(), 1):
    print(f"   {i:2d}. {row['Category']:40s} (Index: {row['Anomaly_Index']:6.2f})")

# Combine for analysis
target_categories = top_surge + bottom_slump
print(f"\n   Total target categories for DiD analysis: {len(target_categories)}")

# ============================================================================
# FILTER PURCHASES TO TARGET PERIOD AND CATEGORIES
# ============================================================================
print("\n4. Filtering purchases to target period and categories:")

# Filter to Jan-March 2020
period_purchases = purchase_df[
    (purchase_df['Order Date'] >= baseline_start) &
    (purchase_df['Order Date'] <= treatment_end)
].copy()

# Filter to target categories
target_purchases = period_purchases[
    period_purchases['Category'].isin(target_categories)
].copy()

print(f"   Purchases in target categories: {len(target_purchases)}")

# Calculate total spend per transaction
target_purchases['Total_Spend'] = (
    target_purchases['Purchase Price Per Unit'] * target_purchases['Quantity']
)

# Assign period labels
def assign_period(date):
    if baseline_start <= date <= baseline_end:
        return 'baseline'
    elif treatment_start <= date <= treatment_end:
        return 'treatment'
    else:
        return 'other'

target_purchases['Period'] = target_purchases['Order Date'].apply(
    lambda x: assign_period(x.strftime('%Y-%m-%d'))
)

# Verify periods
print(f"\n   Purchases by period:")
period_counts = target_purchases['Period'].value_counts()
for period, count in period_counts.items():
    print(f"   - {period}: {count}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE INTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Total_Spend)
# ============================================================================
print("\n5. Creating intensive margin aggregated data:")

# Aggregate total spend per user-category-period
intensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).agg({
    'Total_Spend': 'sum'
}).reset_index()

print(f"   Intensive margin records: {len(intensive_agg)}")
print(f"   Unique users: {intensive_agg['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   Unique categories: {intensive_agg['Category'].nunique()}")

# Fill in missing user-category-period combinations with 0 spend
# First, create all possible combinations
all_users = features_df['Survey ResponseID'].unique()
all_periods = ['baseline', 'treatment']

# Create full grid
intensive_full = []
for user in all_users:
    for category in target_categories:
        for period in all_periods:
            intensive_full.append({
                'Survey ResponseID': user,
                'Category': category,
                'Period': period
            })

intensive_full_df = pd.DataFrame(intensive_full)

# Merge with actual data
intensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    intensive_agg,
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Total_Spend with 0
intensive_final['Total_Spend'] = intensive_final['Total_Spend'].fillna(0)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"   Zero-spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Non-zero spend records: {(intensive_final['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")

# Save intensive margin data
intensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_intensive.csv'
intensive_final.to_csv(intensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {intensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# CREATE EXTENSIVE MARGIN DATA (Has_Purchase)
# ============================================================================
print("\n6. Creating extensive margin aggregated data:")

# Create binary indicator for whether user purchased in category-period
extensive_agg = target_purchases.groupby([
    'Survey ResponseID',
    'Category',
    'Period'
]).size().reset_index(name='transaction_count')

extensive_agg['Has_Purchase'] = 1

print(f"   Extensive margin records (actual purchases): {len(extensive_agg)}")

# Merge with full grid
extensive_final = intensive_full_df.merge(
    extensive_agg[['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period', 'Has_Purchase']],
    on=['Survey ResponseID', 'Category', 'Period'],
    how='left'
)

# Fill missing Has_Purchase with 0
extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] = extensive_final['Has_Purchase'].fillna(0).astype(int)

print(f"   After filling missing combinations: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"   No purchase (0): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 0).sum()}")
print(f"   Has purchase (1): {(extensive_final['Has_Purchase'] == 1).sum()}")

# Save extensive margin data
extensive_path = output_dir / 'user_category_period_aggregated_extensive.csv'
extensive_final.to_csv(extensive_path, index=False)
print(f"\n   ✓ Saved: {extensive_path}")

# ============================================================================
# VERIFY DATA STRUCTURE
# ============================================================================
print("\n7. Verifying data structure:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {intensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {intensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {intensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {intensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2} = {len(all_users)} users × {len(target_categories)} categories × 2 periods")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(intensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample intensive data:")
print(intensive_final.head(10))

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN:")
print(f"   - Shape: {extensive_final.shape}")
print(f"   - Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")
print(f"   - Survey ResponseIDs: {extensive_final['Survey ResponseID'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Categories: {extensive_final['Category'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Periods: {extensive_final['Period'].nunique()}")
print(f"   - Expected rows: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")
print(f"   - Actual rows: {len(extensive_final)}")

print(f"\n   Sample extensive data:")
print(extensive_final.head(10))

# ============================================================================
# SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================================================
print("\n8. Summary statistics:")

print(f"\n   INTENSIVE MARGIN (Total_Spend):")
intensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'median', 'sum', 'std'])
print(intensive_by_period)

print(f"\n   EXTENSIVE MARGIN (Has_Purchase rate):")
extensive_by_period = intensive_final.groupby('Period')['Has_Purchase'].agg(['mean', 'sum'])
print(extensive_by_period)

# By category type
surge_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(top_surge)]
slump_intensive = intensive_final[intensive_final['Category'].isin(bottom_slump)]

print(f"\n   SURGE CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(surge_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

print(f"\n   SLUMP CATEGORIES - Intensive margin:")
print(slump_intensive.groupby('Period')['Total_Spend'].agg(['mean', 'sum']))

# ============================================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*80)
print("DATA PREPARATION COMPLETE - SUMMARY")
print("="*80)

print(f"\nTARGET CATEGORIES:")
print(f"  - Surge categories: {len(top_surge)}")
print(f"  - Slump categories: {len(bottom_slump)}")
print(f"  - Total: {len(target_categories)}")

print(f"\nOUTPUT FILES:")
print(f"  - Intensive margin: {intensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(intensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {intensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\n  - Extensive margin: {extensive_path}")
print(f"    Rows: {len(extensive_final)}")
print(f"    Columns: {extensive_final.columns.tolist()}")

print(f"\nDATA STRUCTURE:")
print(f"  - Users: {len(all_users)}")
print(f"  - Categories: {len(target_categories)}")
print(f"  - Periods: 2 (baseline, treatment)")
print(f"  - Expected rows per file: {len(all_users) * len(target_categories) * 2}")

print(f"\nPURCHASE BEHAVIOR:")

print(f"  - Treatment purchases: {(intensive_final[intensive_final['Period'] == 'treatment']['Total_Spend'] > 0).sum()}")
